compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Context
The authenticated-authorship merge-gate (`verify-identity`) is live but ADVISORY: `identity.require_authenticated=false` and the store holds 0 signed events. To make the gate actually enforce, write-signing must be rolled out first.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Configure self-identity + `identity.signing_key` on the write paths so new events are signed.
- [ ] Confirm new events verify (`rebar verify-identity` shows `verified` for post-boundary events).
- [ ] Flip `identity.require_authenticated=true` in the SAME change that enables signing (historical events already grandfathered by `identity.enforce_since`).
- [ ] Depends on the perf fix (1cc0) so the enforcing gate is fast enough for CI.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Write-signing enabled and producing signed events.
- [ ] `identity.require_authenticated` flipped on with the grandfather boundary intact.
- [ ] `verify-identity` enforces on new unsigned/forged events and passes on signed ones.
compiled_state.title: Roll out authenticated write-signing + flip identity.require_authenticated to enforce
